behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is local module handoff: commit-delta/age computation plus PR-presence state combined into one decision. This is more than a single pure function, so Unit is too low. I considered System, but rejected it because the behavior text does not require live repo/real network ('live' is only explicit in behavior 4), so in-process fixtures for branch metadata + PR state can expose real wiring bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the staleness detector with real local modules and deterministic fixtures representing: commits ahead of main, commit ages around threshold, and PR-open=false. Assert stale=true only when all three conditions hold, including threshold edge cases.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: The risk is orchestration between startup flow, classifier output, and warning renderer/context injection (branch name, age, next action). Unit is too low because this crosses modules. I considered System, but rejected it because behavior text does not require real shell/session bootstrap process; in-process startup invocation catches the actual handoff failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start setup in-process with a detector/classifier result marked stale and verify emitted warning payload includes actionable branch context fields and expected stale messaging.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is explicitly one local deterministic mapping function with no I/O or cross-module boundary. Higher levels are unnecessary because the failure is isolated to local branching logic.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test of classifier inputs to exact labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`), including boundary combinations and determinism checks across repeated calls.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires live repo and PR state, so real git/FS plus real PR-state contract behavior are part of the failure boundary. Integration fakes could hide command/API contract mismatches and rendering fed by real-world state.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo setup with real branch history and real PR state source, then assert rendered output shows `clean` vs `STALE` correctly for each scenario.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is state interpretation across modules (PR state -> stale classifier input -> label). Unit is too low because it depends on handoff. I considered System, but rejected it because behavior text does not require live PR API; deterministic PR-state fixtures (`open`, `merged`) are sufficient to catch false-positive logic.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate PR-state adapter outputs with staleness classifier and assert branches with PR state `open` or `merged` never render/classify as `stale-no-pr`, including mixed age/commit-ahead conditions.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git history semantics (rebase/squash rewrite commit IDs and ancestry), which are OS/filesystem/subprocess behaviors. In-process mocks of commit graphs can miss actual git edge cases, so MOCK-HIDE pushes this to System.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, create stale-looking branch history, then perform actual rebase/squash-merge flows that rewrite hashes; rerun detection and assert branch is not classified `stale-no-pr` once work is represented in main/PR history.
